Scottie Scheffler reaches par-5 No.
13 in two, makes birdie at Travelers
(AP) — Scottie Scheffler made an 8-foot par putt on the 18th hole Sunday and pumped his fist with more emotion than he showed all day, just for the right to return Monday at the rain-delayed Travelers Championship to face a sudden-death playoff against Viktor Hovland.
The final round was stopped for 90 minutes as storms moved over the TPC River Highlands, and officials deemed there was not enough daylight for them to start the playoff.
Monday was the first time a PGA TOUR event went an extra day since THE PLAYERS Championship last year."},{"url":"https://sports.yahoo.com/golf/article/after-stunning-scottie-scheffler-miss-viktor-hovland-claims-travelers-championship-in-monday-playoff-131659355.html","title":"After stunning Scottie Scheffler miss, Viktor Hovland claims Travelers Championship in Monday playoff - Yahoo Sports","content":"But Scottie Scheffler rolled a short putt just left, and Viktor Hovland claimed the Travelers Championship in a one-hole Monday morning playoff.
Sunday's final round saw a rain delay of nearly 90 minutes when the leaders were on the 14th hole.
The Travelers marks the end of the PGA Tour's eight Signature Events for 2026.
The Tour's goal is to create more events — at least at the game's top level — with leaderboards like this year's Travelers.
In addition to Hovland and Scheffler, players at the top of the Travelers leaderboard included major winners like Collin Morikawa at -20, Matt Fitzpatrick at -19, and newly crowned U.S.
The PGA Tour now heads to Illinois for the John Deere Classic, and from there to its traditional two-week U.K. jaunt for the Genesis Scottish Open and the Open Championship, held this year at Royal Birkdale."},{"url":"https://golfweek.usatoday.com/story/sports/golf/pga/2026/06/28/travelers-championship-leaderboard-2026-pga-tour-final-round/90646809007/","title":"Travelers Championship 2026 leaderboard, live updates, highlights","content":"Monday, on Golf Channel, to determine the champion of the 2026 event at TPC River Highlands.

Collin Morikawa, Matt Fitzpatrick and Akshay Bhatia are at 18 under.
Wyndham Clark is at 17 under, as is Corey Conners, who is in the clubhouse.
Viktor Hovland and Scottie Scheffler are tied again after one hole.
After making virtually no putts of importance during Saturday's third round, Scottie Scheffler hits one from 31 feet on the fourth for his first birdie of the day.
Tee shot into the rough for the Norwegian results in a bogey on the opening hole.
Low scores are out there Sunday at TPC River Highlands."},{"url":"https://www.golfchannel.com/pga-tour/news/viktor-hovland-wins-travelers-playoff-after-scottie-schefflers-short-miss","title":"Viktor Hovland wins Travelers playoff after Scottie Scheffler's short miss - Golf Channel","content":"An 83-minute rain delay forced the Travelers into a fifth day, as there was no daylight for more golf after Hovland and Scheffler each finished 72 holes at 21 under."},{"url":"https://www.cbssports.com/golf/news/travelers-championship-leaderboard-2026-live-updates-round-4-scores-coverage/live/","title":"2026 Travelers grades: Scottie Scheffler, Viktor Hovland head to playoff atop leaderboard - CBS Sports","content":"At one point on the front nine, there was a stretch of five straight holes where the lead swung between the two, who played together in the final pairing.
After another two-shot exchange among the final duo on No.
10, Scheffler commanded a two-stroke lead as the likes of Morikawa, Wyndham Clark and Matt Fitzpatrick all made their moves.
Just one more day," Clark said on Saturday.
A two-shot swing on the 18th hole Saturday evening saw Viktor Hovland take a one-stroke lead over Scottie Scheffler into the final round."}]}
